text: Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for CEP Boundary.
CEP panels can host HTML/JS UI and call ExtendScript. CEP remains common in installed tooling but should be treated as legacy when planning new long-lived extensions.

- Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
- Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
- If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

- Treat this page as compiled output. Edit the YAML source, not this Markdown file.
- Machine-readable authority lives in `build/artifacts/knowledge_ast.json`.
Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for Encoder Boundary.
Encoding workflows may use Premiere export APIs or Adobe Media Encoder integration. Export plans must specify preset, path, side effects and queue behavior.

- Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
- Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
- If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

- Treat this page as compiled output. Edit the YAML source, not this Markdown file.
- Machine-readable authority lives in `build/artifacts/knowledge_ast.json`.
Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for ExtendScript Boundary.
ExtendScript is the legacy scripting surface used by CEP panels and scripts. It is synchronous and remains important for existing integrations, but new work should evaluate UXP first.

- Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
- Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
- If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

- Treat this page as compiled output. Edit the YAML source, not this Markdown file.
- Machine-readable authority lives in `build/artifacts/knowledge_ast.json`.

- Do not assume methods from After Effects scripting exist in Premiere.
- Do not assume UXP methods exist in ExtendScript.
- Generated JSX must use only verified ExtendScript methods or explicitly mark unverified areas.

Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for QE Boundary.
QE is an unofficial/internal interface used by many scripts. AI agents must treat QE as unstable and require explicit user consent before suggesting QE-dependent workflows.

- Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
- Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
- If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

- Treat this page as compiled output. Edit the YAML source, not this Markdown file.
- Machine-readable authority lives in `build/artifacts/knowledge_ast.json`.
Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for UXP Boundary.
UXP is the modern plugin and scripting platform for Premiere Pro. UXP exposes the Premiere DOM through require("premierepro"); Premiere operations may require asynchronous calls.

- Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
- Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
- If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

- Treat this page as compiled output. Edit the YAML source, not this Markdown file.
- Machine-readable authority lives in `build/artifacts/knowledge_ast.json`.

- Minimum version must be recorded per member when known.
- Async/sync behavior must be recorded per method/property.
- No method may be used in generated code unless it appears in this API surface or is confirmed by a cited experiment.

Evaluate whether an AI agent can design and generate safe Premiere extension code without hallucinating APIs.
The benchmark scores API-layer declaration, method verification, safety planning, version awareness, rollback, and explanation quality.

- API correctness
- Safety compliance
- Version awareness
- Rollback quality
- Code clarity
- Evidence chain

Define how documented facts, reverse-engineered observations, inferred rules and hypotheses are stored without mixing confidence levels.
Every technical claim SHOULD carry a confidence level. AI agents MUST prefer documented and experimentally verified facts over inferred or hypothetical claims.

- Adobe UXP for Premiere Pro official documentation
- Premiere Pro Scripting Guide public compendium
- AdobeDocs UXP Premiere samples

- Use DOCUMENTED for official Adobe documentation or official samples.
- Use REVERSE_ENGINEERED only for reproducible experiments.
- Use INFERRED only when derived from confirmed facts.
- Use HYPOTHESIS only for unverified research notes.

- When confidence is low, say so explicitly.
- Never convert a hypothesis into a production rule without validation.

- Adobe UXP for Premiere Pro official documentation
- Premiere Pro Scripting Guide public compendium
- AdobeDocs UXP Premiere samples

- `references` → `Adobe UXP for Premiere Pro official documentation`
- `references` → `Premiere Pro Scripting Guide public compendium`
- `references` → `AdobeDocs UXP Premiere samples`
